title: 10 best herbal decoctions for jet lag
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for jet lag are concentrated liquid extracts made from a combination of herbs, often used to alleviate the symptoms of jet lag. These decoctions help by regulating sleep patterns, reducing stress and fatigue, and improving digestion, all of which are disrupted during long-distance travel. Examples include peppermint, ginger, and valerian root decoctions, which can be consumed as teas or added to baths for relaxation. By incorporating these herbal remedies into their routine, travelers can improve the quality of their sleep, reduce jet lag symptoms, and enjoy a smoother transition to new time zones.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian decoctions helps with jet lag because it promotes relaxation and reduces stress. When our bodies are subjected to sudden time changes, it can disrupt our natural rhythms and make us feel tired, anxious, or irritable. Valerian's calming properties help regulate the body's internal clock by promoting a sense of calm and tranquility, allowing us to adjust to new sleep schedules more easily. As a result, valerian decoctions can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as insomnia, fatigue, and restlessness, making it an effective natural remedy for travelers.
remedy_properties: Sedative and hypnotic effects: Valerian's sedative and hypnotic properties can help individuals fall asleep faster and sleep more soundly, which is essential for adjusting to new time zones.

Adaptogenic and relaxing effects: Valerian's adaptogenic and relaxing properties can help reduce stress and anxiety associated with traveling, allowing the body to adjust to the new environment and schedule more easily.

Calming effect on the nervous system: Valerian's calming effects on the nervous system can help regulate the body's internal clock and improve sleep quality by reducing symptoms of insomnia and restlessness.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This terpene helps with jet lag by promoting relaxation and reducing stress caused by disrupted sleep patterns.
Valeranone: This sesquiterpene has sedative properties that can help regulate the body's internal clock and improve sleep quality, which can be affected by jet lag.
Isovaleric acid: This fatty acid has a calming effect on the nervous system and may help reduce symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, anxiety, and restlessness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for its sedative and sleep-promoting effects.
Stems: Contain valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that help regulate sleep patterns and reduce anxiety.
Leaves: Also contain valerenic acid, which is believed to help alleviate insomnia and promote relaxation for better sleep.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried valeriana officinalis root and 1 quart of water for decoction.
Combine the dried root with water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 5-10 minutes to release the root's medicinal properties.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a clean container to remove the solids.
Store the decoction in a sealed container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop decoctions helps with jet lag because its unique blend of flavonoids, terpenes, and alkaloids works to regulate the body's internal clock and alleviate symptoms of fatigue. The decoction's calming effects can help ease anxiety and promote relaxation, making it easier to adjust to new time zones. Additionally, maypop's ability to stimulate digestion and improve sleep quality ensures that you'll feel rested and refreshed upon arrival at your destination.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Maypop decoction helps regulate the body's response to stress, including travel-related stress, by promoting balance in the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis, reducing symptoms of fatigue and anxiety.

Sedative and Calming Effects: The sedative compounds present in maypop decoctions, such as flavonoids and saponins, can help calm the nervous system, promoting relaxation and improved sleep quality, which is crucial for recovering from jet lag.

Antioxidant Properties: Maypop decoction's antioxidant properties can help mitigate oxidative stress caused by air travel, which can contribute to fatigue, inflammation, and other symptoms associated with jet lag.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help alleviate jet lag by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can aid in readjusting to a new sleep schedule.
Herniarin: This flavonoid derivative may assist in reducing the effects of jet lag by acting as a natural sedative, promoting better sleep quality, and reducing stress caused by travel.

Isosakuranetin: This flavonoid is believed to play a role in alleviating jet lag by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate the negative effects of travel on the body's internal clock.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the highest concentration of flavonoids and other active compounds responsible for the sedative effects, which help alleviate jet lag symptoms.
Roots: The root of the plant is rich in alkaloids and flavonoids that promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and improve sleep quality, essential for adapting to new time zones.
Fruits: The fruit of Passiflora incarnata, also known as the maypop, contains a high concentration of flavonoids, vitamins, and minerals that contribute to its calming and sleep-promoting properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 grams of dried passiflora incarnata flowers and 1 liter of boiling water.
Steep the herbal material in the boiling water for 5-10 minutes or until it reaches the desired strength.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Discard the solids and let the decoction cool to room temperature for 30 minutes to an hour.
Store the cooled decoction in a dark glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with jet lag because its soothing properties promote relaxation and calmness, easing the body's transition across time zones. The natural antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender work to reduce stress and anxiety caused by travel disruptions. As a gentle sedative, it can help regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of rest, allowing the body to adjust to its new environment more efficiently. This natural remedy supports a healthier adaptation to jet lag, leaving travelers feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Calming and Sedative Effects: Lavender's sedative properties can help regulate sleep-wake cycles, reducing stress and anxiety caused by jet lag, allowing for a more restful night's sleep.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lavender's antioxidants can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can be exacerbated by long flights and time zone changes, while its anti-inflammatory properties can soothe any discomfort or fatigue.

Relaxing and Soothing Effects on the Nervous System: Lavender's calming effects on the nervous system can help ease stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms of jet lag such as irritability, headaches, and fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps regulate the body's circadian rhythms and promotes relaxation, which can aid in adjusting to a new time zone.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has sedative properties and can help reduce stress and anxiety caused by jet lag, promoting a restful night's sleep.
Luteolin: A phenolic compound that has antioxidant properties and can help mitigate the negative effects of jet lag on the body, such as inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lavandula angustifolia leaves are used in decoctions for jet lag due to their calming properties, which help regulate sleep patterns.
Flowers: Lavandula angustifolia flowers are used in decoctions for jet lag as they have a soothing effect, aiding in relaxation and reducing stress caused by travel.
Stems: Lavandula angustifolia stems are used in decoctions for jet lag due to their mild sedative properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of insomnia and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated for best quality.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place or using a food dehydrator to remove moisture completely.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the flowers to release their active compounds and oils.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or glass bottle for consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with jet lag because its soothing properties can calm the mind and body, allowing for a smoother transition across time zones. The apigenin content in chamomile binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. This, in turn, helps regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms, making it easier to adjust to new sleep patterns and wake-up times. As a result, chamomile decoctions can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as insomnia, fatigue, and disorientation, allowing travelers to feel more refreshed and revitalized upon arrival at their destination.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: Chamomile's anxiolytic effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety caused by travel disruptions, promoting relaxation and reducing the body's natural response to jet lag.

Sedative properties: Chamomile's sedative properties can help regulate sleep patterns and improve the quality of sleep, which is crucial for adjusting to a new time zone and combating jet lag symptoms like fatigue and insomnia.

Adaptogenic properties: Chamomile's adaptogenic effects can help the body adapt to changes in its environment, including changes in time zones, reducing stress and promoting resilience against disruptions caused by travel.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: As a flavonoid, apigenin helps with jet lag by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can aid in adapting to new time zones.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate the oxidative stress caused by jet lag and promote better sleep quality.
α-Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, α-bisabolol can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, anxiety, and digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used due to their calming and soothing properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and anxiety.
Leaves: They are often utilized in decoctions to provide a gentle sedative effect and promote relaxation, which is beneficial for individuals experiencing jet lag.
Seeds: Although not as commonly mentioned as flowers, the seeds of Matricaria chamomilla are sometimes used in decoctions to enhance sleep quality and reduce stress related to jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after the dew has dried but before the heat of the day sets in.
Dry the matricaria chamomilla flowers in a warm dry place out of direct sunlight for 1 to 2 weeks.
Steep 1 to 2 teaspoons of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine mesh to remove the plant material and discard the solids.
Drink the decoction 2 to 3 times a day as needed for relief from anxiety and insomnia symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with jet lag because of its adaptogenic properties, which enable it to regulate the body's natural rhythms. The decoction contains glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to balance cortisol levels and promote relaxation, making it easier to adjust to new time zones. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to stimulate the production of melatonin, a hormone responsible for regulating sleep-wake cycles. By promoting a sense of calm and reducing jet lag symptoms, herbal licorice decoctions can help you feel more refreshed and prepared for your travels.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Licorice root has adaptogenic properties, which means it helps the body adapt to stress and changes in environment, such as those caused by crossing time zones, and promotes a sense of balance and calmness.

Gastroprotective effects: Herbal licorice decoctions can help soothe digestive issues commonly associated with jet lag, such as nausea, bloating, and stomach upset, by protecting the mucous membranes in the gut and reducing inflammation.

Hormone-regulating properties: Licorice root contains compounds that can help regulate hormonal imbalances, which are often exacerbated by travel across time zones, and may help alleviate symptoms like fatigue, insomnia, and mood disturbances.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: A saponin glycoside that helps with jet lag by stabilizing the body's circadian rhythms, thereby reducing the impact of time zone changes on the body's internal clock.

Licorice flavonoids: Flavonoids like luteolin, quercetin, and kaempferol have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing fatigue and promoting better sleep quality, crucial for overcoming jet lag.

Liquiritin: A triterpenoid saponin that exhibits anti-stress properties by reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation, which can help the body adjust to the new time zone and reduce jet lag symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots: The most used part of Glycyrrhiza glabra in decoctions for jet lag is its roots due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which helps regulate cortisol levels and alleviate symptoms of jet lag.

Barks: Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also used in decoctions for jet lag due to their ability to balance cortisol levels and promote a sense of well-being, essential for recovering from jet lag.

Flowers: Flowers: The flowers of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make decoctions for jet lag due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate the fatigue and stress associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots of the plant in the fall season when the plant is fully matured.
Wash the roots thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the roots into small pieces and weigh out 5-7 grams for each 250 milliliters of water.
Steep the chopped roots in boiling water for 10-15 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Broad-leaved lime decoctions helps with jet lag because of its unique combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils. These compounds have been shown to regulate circadian rhythms and reduce symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and digestive issues. The decoction's soothing properties can also calm the nervous system, promoting a sense of balance and relaxation. As a result, it can help the body adjust to new time zones more quickly and naturally, making it an effective natural remedy for combating the effects of jet lag.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Broad-leaved lime decoction's antioxidant properties can help mitigate oxidative stress caused by jet lag, which can lead to fatigue, headaches, and mood disturbances.

Adaptogenic properties: The adaptogenic compounds present in broad-leaved lime decoction may help the body adjust to changes in environmental factors, such as time zones, by promoting hormonal balance and reducing stress levels.

Sedative and anti-anxiety properties: The sedative and anti-anxiety effects of broad-leaved lime decoction can help alleviate symptoms like insomnia, restlessness, and anxiety associated with jet lag, allowing for a more comfortable adjustment to new time zones.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with jet lag by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can aid in adjusting to a new sleep-wake cycle.
Triterpenes (specifically Ursolic Acid): Triterpenes like Ursolic Acid have antioxidant properties that can help reduce fatigue and stress associated with jet lag.

Phenylethanoid Glycosides (specifically Tiliroside): These compounds may help with jet lag by reducing anxiety and promoting a sense of calm, which can be beneficial for travelers adjusting to a new time zone.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make decoctions for their sedative and calming effects, which may help with jet lag symptoms.
Buds: Used to make decoctions for their expectorant properties, which may help alleviate congestion and fatigue associated with jet lag.
Barks: Used to make decoctions for their anti-inflammatory and sedative properties, which may help with sleep disturbances and stress caused by jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried tilia platyphyllos flowers and leaves.
Combine the gathered herbs in a saucepan and add 2 liters of cold water.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 10-15 minutes to facilitate infusion.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 5-7 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a clean container to remove solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with jet lag because its calming properties help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress caused by time zone changes. The soothing aroma of lemon balm also promotes relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep in a new environment. Additionally, its mild sedative effects can help alleviate anxiety and irritability that often accompany jet lag. By sipping on a warm cup of lemon balm tea before bedtime, travelers can gently adjust their body's internal clock and wake up feeling refreshed and rejuvenated upon arrival at their destination.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress caused by travel, which can exacerbate symptoms of jet lag.

Adaptogenic effects: Lemon balm's adaptogenic properties help the body to adjust to new environmental conditions, reducing feelings of fatigue, anxiety, and disorientation that often accompany jet lag.

Calming and sedative effects: The essential oils in lemon balm have a calming and sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate sleep patterns and promote relaxation after long-distance travel.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps alleviate jet lag symptoms by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation caused by disrupted circadian rhythms.
Linalool: As a terpene, linalool promotes relaxation and improves sleep quality by interacting with the body's GABA receptors, which helps to regulate the sleep-wake cycle.
Limonene: This monoterpene has a mood-boosting effect and can help regulate the body's internal clock by influencing the expression of genes involved in circadian rhythm regulation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Melissa officinalis leaves are commonly used due to their high content of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have sedative and calming properties to aid in sleep and relaxation.
Stems: Melissa officinalis stems are used in decoctions due to their ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.
Roots: Melissa officinalis roots are used for their adaptogenic properties, helping the body to adapt to new environments and rhythms, and also for their calming effects on the nervous system.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-4 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves and flowers from a trusted supplier.
Combine the herb with 200-250 ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbal compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats decoctions helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation, calmness, and a sense of grounding. The soothing properties of oats can help to ease the stress and anxiety that often accompanies travel across time zones. As our bodies adjust to new rhythms, oats decoctions can provide a gentle and calming influence, helping to regulate our sleep patterns and reduce the symptoms of jet lag. Additionally, oats are rich in fiber and antioxidants, which can also support overall well-being and vitality during periods of transition.
remedy_properties: Soothing and anti-inflammatory properties: Oat decoctions contain avenanthramides, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues, helping to alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and digestive discomfort.

Calming and sedative effects: The flavonoids present in oats, particularly avenastrin and avenanthramides, have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress caused by time zone changes.

Antioxidant and protective properties: Oat decoctions contain antioxidants such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, which help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by jet lag, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Avenacoside: Avenacoside, a saponin glycoside found in Avena sativa, has been shown to have sedative and anti-anxiety properties, which may help individuals adjust to new sleep schedules and reduce jet lag symptoms.

Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid, a phenolic compound present in Avena sativa, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help mitigate the negative effects of jet lag on the body, such as oxidative stress and inflammation.

Lignans: Lignans, a type of phytoestrogen found in Avena sativa, have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-anxiety effects, which may help reduce stress and promote relaxation, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They contain a higher concentration of nutrients and saponins, which are believed to help alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Roots: They are thought to have a balancing effect on the body's internal clock and promote relaxation.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress caused by jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of avena sativa roots and 2 parts of leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Cut the avena sativa into small pieces to release their active compounds for infusion.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the cut avena sativa with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half its original volume.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose decoctions helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation, reduce stress, and regulate the body's natural rhythms. The calming properties of beach rose help to ease the discomfort and fatigue that often accompanies time zone changes. By promoting a sense of tranquility and calm, beach rose decoctions can help your body adjust more easily to its new surroundings, reducing the symptoms of jet lag and allowing for a smoother transition into your new environment.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: The decoction helps the body adapt to new time zones by regulating cortisol levels and promoting relaxation, reducing stress and anxiety associated with jet lag.

Sedative and calming effects: The beach rose contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have a sedative effect on the nervous system, promoting a restful night's sleep and helping to reduce insomnia caused by jet lag.

Antioxidant properties: The decoction's antioxidant activity helps protect the body from oxidative stress caused by travel-related inflammation and fatigue, reducing symptoms such as headaches, muscle soreness, and lethargy.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help counteract oxidative stress caused by jet lag-related changes in circadian rhythms and sleep patterns.

Rosmarinic acid: As a polyphenolic compound, rosmarinic acid has been shown to have a regulating effect on the body's internal clock and can help mitigate the effects of jet lag by stabilizing circadian rhythm.
Geraniol: This terpene has been found to have a sedative and hypnotic effect, which might help promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.
remedy_recipe: Gather rosa x rugosa flowers and leaves in a quantity of 30 grams for every 1 liter of water.
Dry the gathered plant material in a well-ventilated area for a period of 2 to 4 weeks.
Combine 30 grams of the dried plant material with 1 liter of water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 5 to 10 minutes then reduce heat for a further 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a fine mesh and discard the solids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosa x rugosa roots are used due to their adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to changes in environment and rhythm.
Leaves: Rosa x rugosa leaves are used because they contain antioxidants and flavonoids that can help regulate the body's internal clock and reduce fatigue associated with jet lag.
Flowers: Rosa x rugosa flowers are used due to their calming effects and ability to promote relaxation, which can aid in readjusting to a new time zone.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese date decoctions helps with jet lag because they promote natural sleep-wake cycles and calmness. The herbal blend, often combining dates, licorice root, and other soothing ingredients, gently adjusts the body's internal clock to local time zones. As the warm, comforting liquid is sipped, it calms the mind and reduces stress, allowing the body to relax and prepare for a restful night's sleep. With regular use, jet lag symptoms such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation begin to subside, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: The combination of herbs in a Chinese date decoction helps the body adapt to changes in environment and time zone, reducing stress and fatigue caused by jet lag.

Sedative and calming effects: The decoction's sedative properties can help regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, promoting relaxation and improving the quality of sleep during travel or after arrival at a new destination.

Digestive soothing properties: The herbs in the decoction can help alleviate digestive discomforts such as constipation, diarrhea, or nausea often experienced during air travel, which can further exacerbate jet lag symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds may help mitigate jet lag by reducing inflammation and improving sleep quality due to their antioxidant properties.
Saponins: Saponins in Ziziphus jujuba may aid in regulating circadian rhythms and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate jet lag symptoms.
Phenolic acids: These compounds may help stabilize the body's internal clock and reduce oxidative stress, contributing to improved sleep and reduced jet lag symptoms.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used due to their adaptogenic properties, which help regulate the body's internal clock and reduce fatigue associated with jet lag.
Barks: The barks are utilized for their calming effects, which can help alleviate stress and promote a restful sleep, crucial for recovering from jet lag.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by long-distance travel and time zone changes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried ziziphus jujuba fruit from a trusted source and clean it thoroughly.
Crush the dried fruit into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 2g of the powder with 1 liter of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes and then strain it through a cheesecloth into a separate container.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator and consume 20-30ml of it 2-3 times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Monk's pepper decoctions helps with jet lag because of its unique ability to regulate the body's circadian rhythms. The decoction contains a compound called magnoflorine, which has been shown to synchronize the body's natural sleep-wake cycle with the environment. This allows travelers to adjust quickly to new time zones and reduces the symptoms of jet lag, including fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation. By taking monk's pepper decoctions before and after travel, individuals can minimize the discomfort of jet lag and feel more refreshed upon arrival at their destination.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic property: Monk's pepper decoctions have an adaptogenic effect, which helps the body adjust to stress and fatigue caused by jet lag, allowing it to adapt more easily to new time zones.

Circadian rhythm regulating properties: The decoction contains compounds that can regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms, helping to synchronize them with the new timezone, reducing symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia, disorientation, and fatigue.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Monk's pepper decoctions are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by jet lag, promoting overall physical and mental well-being.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and adaptogenic properties, making them effective in calming the nervous system and reducing stress caused by jet lag.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, headache, and digestive issues often associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Gather one cup of dried cnidium monnieri roots and two cups of water in a large pot.
Boil the mixture for ten minutes on high heat, then reduce the heat to low for twenty minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Store the decoction in an airtight container and refrigerate it for up to three days or freeze it for later use.
Consume one cup of the cooled decoction twice daily, morning and night, for seven consecutive days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stevia decoctions helps with jet lag because they promote a natural adjustment to new time zones. The herbal blend contains adaptogenic properties that calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of fatigue and disorientation. As the body absorbs the stevia's natural compounds, it begins to synchronize its internal rhythms with the new environment, helping to alleviate symptoms such as insomnia, digestive issues, and mood disturbances. By calming the body's response to travel stress, stevia decoctions enable a smoother transition into local time, making it easier to adjust to a new schedule.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Stevia's adaptogenic properties help the body adjust to stress and fatigue caused by time zone changes, reducing symptoms like insomnia and irritability.
Caffeine-free energy boost: Stevia's natural sweetener properties provide a gentle, non-caffeinated energy boost that can help combat lethargy and morning grogginess often experienced during jet lag.
Stress-reducing flavonoids: Stevia contains flavonoid compounds that have been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality – essential for overcoming jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and adaptogenic properties, helping to reduce stress and promote relaxation.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue and body aches.
remedy_recipe: Harvest stevia rebaudiana leaves from a trusted source or purchase them in dried form for decoction preparation.
Measure 2-3 teaspoons of dried stevia rebaudiana leaves per cup of filtered water and add to a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes to release herbal compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding solids.
Store the cooled stevia rebaudiana decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with jet lag because they contain a unique blend of adaptogenic compounds that help regulate the body's internal clock. The decoction's calming effects can soothe symptoms of fatigue, irritability, and disorientation that often accompany long-haul travel. Additionally, the herbal ingredients may aid in adjusting the body's circadian rhythms by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep despite changes in time zones.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Maidenhair Tree decoctions have been shown to help the body adapt to stress and environmental changes, which can be beneficial in reducing symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation.

Antioxidant activity: The decoction contains antioxidants that can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by air travel, which can contribute to symptoms like fatigue, muscle soreness, and mood disturbances.
Calming effects: Maidenhair Tree has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate anxiety, irritability, and restlessness often experienced during jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree have been traditionally used to make decoctions that help regulate the body's internal clock and alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue and insomnia.

Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that are believed to have a calming effect on the nervous system, making it easier for the body to adjust to new time zones and reduce symptoms of jet lag.

Seeds: The seeds of the maidenhair tree are rich in antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that help to protect the body from oxidative stress caused by long-distance travel and disrupt sleep patterns, a common symptom of jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried ginkgo biloba leaves and boiling water.
Heat the water in a saucepan on medium heat.
Add the ginkgo biloba leaves to the hot water.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink the ginkgo biloba decoction at bedtime.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with jet lag because its unique blend of flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds work in harmony to soothe and regulate the body's natural rhythms. The adaptogenic properties of elder allow it to gently calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety caused by travel disruptions. Additionally, elder's antioxidant properties help protect against inflammation and oxidative damage, which can exacerbate jet lag symptoms like fatigue and insomnia. By promoting a sense of balance and well-being, elder decoctions support the body's natural ability to adjust to new time zones.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and saponins, which help to regulate sleep-wake cycles and reduce stress caused by jet lag.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, which aid in calming the nervous system and promoting relaxation to alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Gently rinse 1-2 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers with boiling water for 30 seconds.
Combine the rinsed flowers with 250 milliliters of cold water in a clean glass container.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, to create a decoction.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Consume the decoction immediately after preparation, warmed or at room temperature, as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail decoctions helps with jet lag because of its unique ability to balance the body's natural circadian rhythms. The rich source of silicates, silica, and other minerals in horsetail decoctions helps to regulate the body's internal clock, reducing symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation. Additionally, horsetail's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adjust to new time zones more efficiently, promoting a smooth transition into the local sleep-wake cycle.
remedy_properties: Mineral-rich properties: Field horsetail decoctions are rich in minerals such as silicon, potassium, and manganese, which can help replenish and balance the body's mineral levels after long flights and time zone changes.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in field horsetail decoctions may help reduce fatigue, joint pain, and other symptoms associated with jet lag, making it easier to adjust to a new sleep-wake cycle.

Adaptogenic properties: Field horsetail is considered an adaptogen, which means it can help the body adapt to stressors such as travel and time changes by reducing cortisol levels, promoting relaxation, and regulating the body's natural rhythms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the field horsetail plant are rich in minerals and antioxidants, making them an excellent addition to a jet lag remedy as they help to restore balance and calmness.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the field horsetail plant contain a high concentration of silica, which can help to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce symptoms of fatigue and disorientation associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried equisetum arvense herb in 20-30 gram quantities for each dose.
Combine the herb with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Consume the resulting decoction three times daily, 30 minutes before meals, in dosages of 50-100 milliliters.
Store any remaining decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours and reheat as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with jet lag because they promote digestive balance and reduce inflammation, which can be disrupted by air travel. The antioxidants and vitamins in dandelion root help regulate body rhythms and reduce symptoms of fatigue, nausea, and insomnia that often accompany jet lag. Additionally, the bitter properties of dandelion root stimulate digestion and increase gut motility, helping to alleviate bloating and discomfort caused by changes in diet and environment during long flights.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion helps to flush out excess fluids and toxins from the body, which can be beneficial in combating the bloating and water retention that often accompanies jet lag.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with jet lag, such as headaches, fatigue, and joint pain.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by travel-related changes in diet, environment, and sleep patterns, which can exacerbate jet lag symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, dandelion roots help alleviate fatigue and improve sleep quality.
Buds: Containing high levels of flavonoids and terpenes, dandelion buds can help regulate body clocks and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried taraxacum officinale roots and flowers.
Boil one liter of water in a saucepan for exactly 15 minutes.
Add the taraxacum officinale mixture to the boiling water for 20 minutes steeping time.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a glass container.
Allow the decoction to cool down to room temperature before consuming one cup three times daily.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jet lag that are treated with herbal decoctions are those caused by disruptions in the body's natural rhythms, such as insomnia, fatigue, and digestive issues. Herbal remedies like valerian root, chamomile, and lavender can help calm the mind and promote relaxation, while others like ashwagandha, ginseng, and ginger can boost energy levels and reduce inflammation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with jet lag is a blend of Peppermint, Ginger, and Ashwagandha. Peppermint soothes digestive issues and calms the mind, while Ginger warms the body and aids in circulation. Ashwagandha, an adaptogenic herb, helps regulate the body's internal clock and reduces stress. Combine these decoctions in equal parts, steep them in hot water for 5-7 minutes, and drink before bedtime or upon waking to help alleviate jet lag symptoms and promote a smooth transition to a new time zone.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis decoctions helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation and regulate the body's internal clock. The valerenic acid present in Valeriana officinalis has a sedative effect, which can aid in falling asleep and reducing sleep disruptions caused by jet lag. Additionally, it has a balancing effect on the nervous system, helping to readjust the body's circadian rhythms after a long-distance travel. This results in improved sleep quality and reduced fatigue, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This constituent helps with jet lag by promoting relaxation and reducing stress caused by time zone changes, allowing for easier adaptation to new sleep schedules.
Valeranone: Valeranone contributes to alleviating jet lag symptoms by regulating the body's internal clock and promoting a natural sleep-wake cycle.
Valeranone acetate: Valeranone acetate helps mitigate jet lag effects by exhibiting sedative properties, which can aid in falling asleep and improving the quality of sleep after long-distance travel.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of valerenic acid and other active compounds, which help to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
Stems: Valerian stems contain valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce stress and anxiety, making them a useful addition to jet lag decoctions.
Leaves: Valerian leaves are used in some decoctions for their calming and sedative properties, which can help to alleviate jet lag symptoms such as insomnia and restlessness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of dried roots of valeriana officinalis from a trusted supplier or source.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and sift them through a fine-mesh sieve to remove debris.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the chopped dried roots with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid has cooled to room temperature slowly.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container to remove the solids completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata decoctions helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation and reduce stress associated with travel. The herb contains compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids, which have sedative and anxiolytic properties. By calming the nervous system, Passiflora incarnata decoctions can help regulate the body's internal clock and improve sleep quality. This can be particularly beneficial for travelers who experience disrupted sleep patterns due to time zone changes, allowing them to adjust more quickly to their new environment.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help alleviate jet lag by reducing stress and anxiety caused by disrupted circadian rhythms, promoting relaxation and a good night's sleep.
Harman and Norharman alkaloids: Aid in managing jet lag by modulating neurotransmitters that regulate sleep-wake cycles, promoting a stable sleep-wake balance.

Isovitexin and Orobol glucosides: Assist in mitigating the effects of jet lag by exhibiting sedative properties and helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by travel-related fatigue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make decoctions as they contain the highest concentration of passiflorin, a key compound responsible for the plant's sedative properties.
Stems: The stems are used in decoctions for their calming effects, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with jet lag, such as insomnia and restlessness.
Roots: The roots are used in decoctions for their sedative and antispasmodic properties, which can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce anxiety related to jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of fresh or dried root of the flowering vine in the morning when the dew is still present.
Cut the harvested root into smaller pieces and clean them thoroughly with a soft brush to remove any debris.
Boil 1 quart of water in a pot and add the prepared root pieces to the boiling water.
Simmer the mixture for 5-10 minutes to release the active compounds from the root into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove the root particles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia decoctions helps with jet lag because it promotes relaxation and regulates the body's internal clock. The calming properties of lavender help to reduce stress and anxiety associated with travel, allowing the body to adjust to a new time zone more efficiently. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in lavender also help to regulate the body's circadian rhythms, ensuring a restful sleep and reducing the effects of jet lag. This leads to improved sleep quality and reduced fatigue.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps with jet lag by promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and regulating sleep patterns due to its sedative properties.
Linalyl acetate: Contributes to alleviating jet lag symptoms by promoting a sense of calmness, reducing anxiety, and improving sleep quality due to its anxiolytic and sedative effects.

Caryophyllene oxide: Aids in mitigating jet lag by reducing inflammation, promoting relaxation, and regulating the body's circadian rhythms due to its anti-inflammatory and sleep-regulating properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their calming and relaxing properties to help alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Flowers: Lavandula angustifolia flowers are used for their soothing effects on the nervous system, promoting better sleep and reducing jet lag.
Stems: Lavandula angustifolia stems contain essential oils that can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers from a reputable source and clean them thoroughly.
Combine 1 tablespoon of the cleaned flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container to remove solids.
Store the resulting herbal decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis decoctions helps with jet lag because they contain adaptogenic properties that help regulate the body's internal clock. The herbal remedy has been used traditionally to reduce stress and anxiety associated with travel. Its calming effects promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, which is essential for adjusting to a new time zone. Additionally, Melissa officinalis is believed to stimulate the production of melatonin, a hormone that regulates sleep-wake cycles, thus making it easier to adapt to the new time zone.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps with jet lag by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, making it easier to adjust to a new sleep schedule.
Rosmarinic acid: As a phenolic compound, rosmarinic acid aids in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to fatigue and disrupted sleep patterns associated with jet lag.

Volatile oil: The volatile oil in Melissa officinalis, rich in linalool and other terpenes, helps regulate sleep patterns by promoting a sense of calm and normalizing the body's natural sleep-wake cycle.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used for their calming and sedative properties, helping to regulate sleep patterns.
Stems: They contain a high concentration of the calming compounds found in the plant, making them effective in treating insomnia and promoting relaxation.
Roots: They have a grounding effect, helping to regulate the body's internal clock and promote restful sleep.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25-30 grams of melissa officinalis leaves and flowers dried in a cool dark place.
Combine the dried melissa officinalis with 1 liter of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid reaches a temperature of 95-100 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator and consume within 3 days after preparation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba decoctions helps with jet lag because they promote improved circulation and cognitive function. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in ginkgo biloba may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with travel fatigue. Additionally, ginkgo biloba's ability to enhance blood flow to the brain may aid in regulating the body's natural circadian rhythms, allowing for a smoother transition to a new time zone. This can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue and disorientation.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with jet lag by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the brain, which can help alleviate fatigue and promote better sleep.
Terpenoids: They assist in regulating the body's internal clock by influencing the production of melatonin and other hormones, thus aiding in the adjustment to a new time zone.
Bilobalide: It reduces oxidative stress and improves cognitive function, which can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as confusion, disorientation, and difficulty concentrating.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are commonly used in decoctions due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help alleviate jet lag symptoms.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used in decoctions for their potential to regulate circadian rhythms and improve sleep quality, which can be disrupted by jet lag.
Buds: Ginkgo biloba buds are sometimes used in decoctions for their ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can contribute to jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves from a trusted source on a dry morning.
Chop the ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area.
Combine the chopped leaves with 1 liter of boiling water in a large pot.
Simmer the mixture for 30 to 40 minutes to release the active compounds slowly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa decoctions helps with jet lag because they promote relaxation and regulate the body's internal clock. The plant's calming properties help to reduce stress and anxiety associated with travel across time zones. Avena sativa also contains tryptophan, an amino acid that converts into serotonin and melatonin, hormones essential for regulating sleep-wake cycles. By promoting a sense of calm and regulating the body's natural rhythms, Avena sativa decoctions can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.
remedy_constituents: Avenanthramides: These polyphenolic compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by jet lag, promoting a more balanced circadian rhythm.
Stilbene glycosides: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help mitigate the negative effects of jet lag on the body, such as fatigue and insomnia.
Ferulic acid: This phenolic acid helps regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle by reducing inflammation and promoting the production of melatonin, a hormone essential for regulating sleep.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Known for their sleep-promoting and calming properties, which help mitigate the effects of jet lag.
Leaves: Used for their sedative and relaxing effects, which can aid in adjusting to new time zones and promoting restful sleep.
Roots: Utilized for their adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to changes in environment and circadian rhythms, reducing jet lag symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest avena sativa roots and rhizomes by digging them up from the ground when they are mature.
Dry the harvested roots and rhizomes by spreading them out in a single layer on a surface for 2 weeks.
Crush the dried roots and rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to increase their surface area.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of the powder with 2 cups of boiling water in a saucepan and reduce heat to simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine mesh to remove the solids and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale decoctions helps with jet lag because they contain properties that aid in regulating the body's internal clock and reducing fatigue. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been found to influence the body's circadian rhythms, promoting a sense of balance and well-being. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate any discomfort associated with jet lag, making it easier to adjust to new time zones and recover from long-distance travel. This can lead to a smoother transition and reduced jet lag symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These lipophilic compounds help alleviate symptoms of jet lag by reducing nausea and inflammation caused by rapid travel across time zones.
Shogaols: Shogaols have anti-inflammatory properties that help counteract the physical strain and fatigue associated with jet lag.
Gingerols and Shogaols' precursor, Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has antioxidant properties that help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by changes in time zones and travel.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The main component used, as it contains the most active compounds responsible for its medicinal properties.
Stems: Used in some decoctions to enhance the flavor and aroma of the rhyzomes.
Leaves: Added to some recipes to increase the potency of the decoction and provide additional health benefits.
remedy_recipe: Grind 1 teaspoon of dried zingiber officinale root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground zingiber officinale root powder with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5 to 7 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the decoction using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids.
Drink the herbal zingiber officinale decoction immediately after straining while still warm.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng decoctions helps with jet lag because they can help regulate the body's circadian rhythms and alleviate fatigue associated with long-distance travel. The ginsenosides present in Panax ginseng have been shown to increase alertness and improve sleep quality, which is particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing jet lag. Additionally, Panax ginseng decoctions have been found to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, further contributing to its potential in mitigating the negative effects of jet lag on the body.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins help alleviate jet lag by regulating the body's circadian rhythms and improving sleep quality.
Dammarane saponins: These compounds contribute to the antifatigue and adaptogenic effects of Panax ginseng, helping to mitigate the physical and mental exhaustion caused by jet lag.
Panaxynol: This lignan helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can be triggered by changes in time zones and disrupted sleep patterns, thereby aiding in the recovery from jet lag.
remedy_parts: Roots: The most commonly used part due to its high concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to help alleviate jet lag symptoms.
Leaves: Used in some decoctions to provide additional ginsenoside content and help regulate the body's internal clock.
Stems: Utilized in some formulations to add to the overall ginsenoside content and help reduce fatigue associated with jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh panax ginseng roots to use for decoction preparation.
Clean the roots thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris.
Slice the cleaned ginseng roots into thin pieces to increase surface area.
Combine 9-15 grams of sliced ginseng roots with 3 liters of water in a pot.
Boil the mixture for 1-2 hours to extract the bioactive compounds.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis decoctions helps with jet lag because it contains quinine, a natural compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help regulate the body's internal clock and reduce the effects of jet lag. The decoction also stimulates the production of melatonin, a hormone that regulates sleep-wake cycles. Additionally, quinine's vasodilatory properties help improve blood circulation, which can alleviate the fatigue and lethargy often associated with jet lag. This natural remedy promotes a smooth transition to a new time zone.
remedy_constituents: Quinine: Helps with jet lag by its antioxidant properties, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by travel across different time zones.
Quinidine: Contributes to alleviating jet lag symptoms by its ability to regulate heart rhythm and improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted due to time zone changes.
Quinovic acid: Assists in mitigating jet lag by its anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce fatigue and improve overall well-being in travelers.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Its leaves are the most widely used part of the plant due to their high content of alkaloids, including quinine, which may help alleviate fatigue associated with jet lag.
Barks: The bark of the Cinchona officinalis contains high levels of caffeine, which can help increase alertness and reduce fatigue, making it a potential remedy for jet lag.
Stems: The stems of the plant may also be used, containing some amount of caffeine and alkaloids that can contribute to the plant's potential effects in alleviating jet lag symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-4 ounces of cinchona officinalis bark in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the bark into small pieces to increase the surface area for infusion.
Combine 2-4 ounces of the chopped bark with 1 quart of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus decoctions helps with jet lag because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids and essential oils that promote relaxation and regulate the body's internal clock. The herb's sedative properties help to reduce stress and anxiety associated with travel, while its antioxidant properties help to adapt the body to new time zones. Additionally, Humulus lupulus has been shown to improve sleep quality, which is essential for recovery from jet lag. This makes it a popular natural remedy for travelers.
remedy_constituents: Lupulones: These tannins help reduce inflammation and improve sleep quality, which can be disrupted by jet lag.
Humulone: This sesquiterpene lactone has sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to a restful night's sleep and alleviating jet lag symptoms.
Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has a sedative effect and can help reduce stress and anxiety associated with jet lag.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of humulone, an alpha acid that can help regulate the body's internal clock.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant contain humulone and other compounds that can help to reduce jet lag symptoms such as fatigue and insomnia.
Stems: The stems of Humulus lupulus also contain humulone and other compounds that can help to regulate the body's circadian rhythms and reduce jet lag symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 2 grams of dried humulus lupulus flowers and place them in a clean glass container.
Combine the dried flowers with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan and stir gently.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Discard the solids and let the decoction cool to room temperature for later use.
